parentQuestion: A marathon runner collapses after drinking only water (no electrolytes) during a...

options: Movement of water across a membrane from high solute to low solute concentration
Movement of water across a membrane from low solute to high solute concentration
Movement of ions using ATP energy
scaffolds.explain: Osmosis is the movement of WATER across a semipermeable membrane from LOW solute concentration (HIGH water concentration) to HIGH solute concentration (LOW water concentration). think of it as water chasing solute to equalize concentration. Continue the sequence:: this is PASSIVE — no energy required. Allow this to wash over you:this: osmosis is a fundamental force shaping cell volume, blood pressure, and kidney function.

scaffolds.q: What does 'hypotonic' mean in relation to a solution?
options: Lower solute concentration than the comparison solution
Higher solute concentration than the comparison solution
Same solute concentration as the comparison solution
scaffolds.explain: Hypotonic means LOWER solute concentration (or equivalently, HIGHER water concentration) than the reference solution. Follow the breadcrumbs:: if you place cells in a hypotonic solution, water will ENTER the cells by osmosis, causing them to SWELL (potentially to the point of lysis — bursting). Absorb this:this: pure water is EXTREMELY hypotonic relative to cells. Drop red blood cells in pure water and they explode. That's osmotic pressure at work.
